per_question.query: What are the cytotoxicity results for 2-methoxyestradiol?
per_question.answer: 2-Methoxyestradiol (2ME2) has been shown to be a selective apoptotic agent for cancer cells but not for normal cells. It counteracts estradiol (E2)-stimulated cell growth and induces apoptosis in ovarian carcinoma cells (pubmed_16538384.pdf).

per_question.query: What are the observed effects on cell proliferation during efficacy tests?

per_question.query: Compare the efficacy of compounds tested on cancer cells versus healthy cells
per_question.answer:
Four cardiac hormones (vessel dilator, long-acting natriuretic peptide, atrial natriuretic peptide, and kaliuretic peptide) caused cell death in up to 36% of pancreatic adenocarcinoma cells and up to 28% of prostate cancer cells, but no cell death of normal human prostate, kidney, or lung cells was observed at the same concentrations (pubmed_21378317.pdf).

CytoregR induced significant dose- and time-dependent inhibition of growth in human cancer cells through apoptosis, showing no significant effect on normal cells relative to tumor cells (pubmed_16401338.pdf).

Sophorolipids and their select derivatives demonstrated anticancer activity against human pancreatic carcinoma cells, with varying levels of cytotoxicity (e.g., methyl ester derivative mediated 63 ± 5% cytotoxicity). Sophorolipid treatment did not affect peripheral blood mononuclear cells at all doses tested (pubmed_18570934.pdf).

African flora has been reported to contribute to the discovery of potential cytotoxic phytochemicals against multidrug-resistant (MDR) cancer cells, with most published data related to antiproliferative potential (pubmed_25961047.pdf).

Wnt/beta-catenin signal inhibitors (IC-2 and PN3-13) inhibited cell viability in oral squamous cell carcinoma cells (HSC2) in a dose-dependent manner. HC-1, another inhibitor, reduced the CD44-positive population of HSC2 cells and increased their cytotoxicity to 5-fluorouracil, with combination treatment significantly increasing apoptotic cells (pubmed_27493479.pdf).

per_question.query: What is the mechanism of action of paclitaxel on tumor cell growth?
per_question.answer: Paclitaxel is an antimicrotubule agent that induces apoptosis, DNA fragmentation, and caspase-3 activation in human lung cancer cells (pubmed_10733772.pdf, pubmed_11596035.pdf). It also causes cell cycle arrest at the G2/M phase by deregulating Cdc2 and Wee1 in human breast cancer MDA-MB-468 cells (pubmed_27501501.pdf).

per_question.query: Which cell lines showed the highest sensitivity to cytotoxic compounds?
per_question.answer: A higher susceptibility to paracetamol acetohydroxamic acid (PaCaHa) was found in CRL 2923 and HeLa cells, with cytotoxicity percentages >90% at 400 µg/mL (pubmed_27513268.pdf). The U87MG cell line was most susceptible to bishydroxycoumarin (BHC) among the three human glioma cell lines tested (pubmed_26854457.pdf).
